title:
Add a /health endpoint to the Go HTTP service. The endpoint should return JSON with:
"status": "ok", "uptime": seconds since server start, "version": Go runtime version.
Include unit tests for the health handler.

Test run: 1780440440128
goal: Add a /health endpoint to the Go HTTP service that returns a JSON response containing the status ('ok'), uptime in seconds, and the Go runtime version. Implement the handler and its registration in main.go, and add unit tests in a new main_test.go file.
context: The project is a simple Go HTTP service. It currently has a root endpoint and some auth utilities. A /health endpoint is needed for monitoring purposes, providing the service status, uptime, and the Go runtime version.

capabilities.name: service-health-check
capabilities.description: Expose a /health endpoint that returns JSON containing the service status, uptime, and Go runtime version.

decisions.title: Health Endpoint Format
decisions.decision: Return JSON containing status, uptime, and Go runtime version
decisions.rationale: Requirement dictates JSON response with specific fields (status, uptime, version) in main.go

success_criteria: Response body is JSON containing status 'ok', uptime, and version

requirements.title: Health endpoint MUST return JSON with status, uptime, and version
requirements.description: The system MUST expose a /health HTTP endpoint. WHEN a client issues a GET request to /health, THEN the system MUST return an HTTP 200 OK response with a JSON body containing "status": "ok", "uptime" as seconds since server start, and "version" as the Go runtime version. The handler MUST be covered by unit tests verifying this behavior.

scenarios.title: Health endpoint returns successful JSON response with required fields
scenarios.given: the HTTP server is initialized with a health handler
scenarios.when: a GET request is made to the health handler function
then: the response status code is 200 OK
the response body contains 'status' equal to 'ok'
the response body contains 'uptime' as a non-negative integer representing seconds since start
the response body contains 'version' matching the Go runtime version

stories.title: Implement /health endpoint
stories.intent: Add a /health endpoint to the Go HTTP service that returns JSON status, uptime, and Go runtime version for monitoring.

tasks.description: Write failing unit tests for the health handler (targeting main_test.go) verifying JSON structure and 200 OK
Initialize a start time variable in main.go to track service uptime from boot
Implement the health handler returning JSON with status 'ok', uptime in seconds, and runtime.Version()
Register the /health endpoint in the main HTTP ServeMux
Verify the /health endpoint returns the expected JSON response via a smoke test
